summary: Monitor count-only for collab ledgers: malformed JSON lines won't crash but counts can be wrong (wc -l).
Telemetry child-agents.jsonl parsing in monitor uses per-line json.loads with try/except and skips malformed lines, so it is tolerant but silently under-reports if corruption occurs.
Smoke test checks raw text and may fail if expected substring line is malformed/truncated.

evidence.note: child_counts parses JSONL per line with try/except
protocol instructs tolerate malformed trailing lines

recommendations: Track and surface invalid/skipped JSONL line counts for telemetry; optionally warn in monitor.
Use robust record counting (parse-valid-lines) for collab ledgers if accuracy matters.
Keep tolerant parsing for any future collab ledger readers; treat missing files as empty.
